summary: Lead accepts M4 task graph after systems_architect REVIEW and independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT. The graph contains 13 M4 task files, 27 M4 dependency edges, no cycles, M4-TASK-GRAPH-001 in ACCEPTED state, and all downstream M4 tasks remain DRAFT pending main integration and lead promotion of the first dependency-ready implementation task. No M4 product systems were implemented.

tests_run.command: taskctl validate all M4 task files

tests_run.result: M4-TASK-GRAPH-001 REVIEW before lead acceptance; downstream M4 tasks DRAFT; ready empty
13 task files, 27 M4 edges, no cycles

decisions: Accepted the M4 DAG for PR integration.
Kept all downstream M4 implementation/validation tasks DRAFT until the task graph enters main and closure promotes the first dependency-ready task.
Preserved exclusions for manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product/architecture changes.
risks: Downstream M4 tasks are mostly R3 due to deterministic simulation, protocol, save/replay, and client read-model surfaces.
M4 stress validation remains milestone evidence rather than final M5 player-study or final H-004 proof.
requested_action: Commit accepted state, open PR, require CI, merge to main, close M4-TASK-GRAPH-001, then promote and start the first dependency-ready M4 implementation task.
